name: Canning Mushrooms (Whole or Sliced)
recipeIngredient: 14-1/2 pounds domestic mushrooms with short stems, tight veils, and no discoloration
recipeInstructions:
1. Select brightly colored, small to medium-size domestic mushrooms.
2. Trim stems and remove any discolored parts.
3. Wash mushrooms in clean water.
4. Leave small mushrooms whole; cut larger ones into halves or quarters.
5. Fill jars with hot mushrooms, leaving 1-inch headspace.
6. Add 1/2 teaspoon of salt per pint to the jar, if desired.
7. For better color retention, add 1/8 teaspoon of ascorbic acid powder or a 500-milligram tablet of vitamin C.
8. Add fresh hot water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
9. Adjust lids and process jars according to Table 1 or Table 2.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for dial-gauge pressure canner
- Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
- Process Time: 45 minutes
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
- 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
- 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb
- 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb
- 6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb
Table 2: Recommended processing time for weighted-gauge pressure canner
- Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
- Process Time: 45 minutes
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
- 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
